meta.page_title: Internet when I was born (from my experience)
meta.source_title: When Internet First appeared in Ukraine?

content.text: Internet when I was born: my experience
Small intro to when I was born.

Let’s start about my life with internet. I was born in small village in 2006. In Kyiv, Kharkiv, Donetsk(I will use Ukrainian pronunciation instead of Russian with Kiev and Kharkov) and some other major cities already have internet and started creating sites, all other only heard of internet or World Wide Web. But let’s talk about some statistic: according to the Bigmir)net portal, the unique audience of the Ukrainian Internet reached 2.418 million users in December 2005.
What it was like (official)

At the beginning of December 2006, the Ukrainian Internet audience reached 3.437 million users. This means the number of Internet users in Ukraine has grown by more than a million, and in relative terms, by almost 50%.

The number of domains (second- and third-level) registered in the .UA territorial domain at the beginning of the year was 175,441. By the beginning of December 2006, this number had reached 234,149. The number of domains on the Ukrainian Internet has grown by almost 60,000, or a third compared to the previous year.
This trend is encouraging: the number of Internet users in Ukraine is growing, and the number of websites is increasing—the Ukrainian Internet is growing, and at a fairly rapid pace.

With that more and more people creating web sites people start use internet with more serious thoughts in mind. Web sites about politics, news, money and just something more than little games started to take other internet.

And here are some sites that took some nomination on different themes. In December, InternetUA magazine named the leaders of the Ukrainian internet. The publication's journalists and experts selected the most dynamic portals and websites, providers' technological achievements, and those who attracted widespread interest with their innovative solutions and active development.
The following received awards
"Portal of the Year" — Online.ua,
"Email Service of the Year" — Ukr.net,
"Search Engine of the Year" — Google.com.ua,
"Update of the Year" — Meta.ua,
"News Resource of the Year" — Korrespondent.net,
"Web Service of the Year" — MP3.ua,
"Startup of the Year" — Gismeteo.ua,
"Community of the Year" — Dynamo.kiev.ua,
"Web Store of the Year" — Petrovka.ua,
"Trading System of the Year" — Price.ua,
"Hosting Provider of the Year" — Volia Data Center,
"Mobile Internet Provider of the Year" — UMC,
and "High-Speed ​​Provider of the Year" — Volia.
Not official stories from people

But now let’s talk about what people felt about internet and it was mixed at best negative at worst. As we saw from previous text there were more and more people using it, feeling it was helpful or just interesting, but there also people who didn’t like it. For example I found pretty stupid message but worth mentioning to show what some people(especially people 30 to 40 plus) thought about that. Please don’t take it seriously!
“Your attitude towards the Internet
Three words that describe the Internet for me (expletives)

When I'm online, I sweat. 3. Most websites on the Internet are crap. 4. Time flies online. 5. The Internet helps me experience the highest pleasure. 6. Most people on the Internet are cool intellectuals. 7. After logging off the Internet, I feel like an idiot. 8. I'm attracted to porn sites and perversions on the Internet. 9. My favorite thing about the Internet is pop-up ads. 10. Other people on the Internet seem smarter than me. 11. When I'm online, I have a hard time getting to the bathroom. 12. All the information on the Internet is valuable and useful. 13. Dead links don't irritate me. 14. When I click links, I feel a slight tingling in my left heel. 15. The Internet doesn't interfere with my life. 16. I visit websites to get sexual satisfaction. 17. The Internet is useless unless you use it. 18. I think the Internet is for sick people. Result: 17 "yes" answers - you are a full user. Up to 9 "yes" answers - you are a half-user. From 0 to 9 "yes" answers - you are a normal person.”
Or

“People treated it like video games—you know, just a niche geek thing that only nerds liked. I never thought it was just a fad, but I also didn't predict it would become such a universal thing that everyone would use, rather than being relegated to the realm of nerds.”
Also I think i need to explain user thing, in Ukrainian user word is  sounds pretty similar to looser. So when this person calls “you a full user” it actually means “you a full looser”.
But there were and more enthusiastic thoughts about internet

“I think it was pretty obvious that the internet would change our lives. We immediately realized how useful it would be for exchanging information and ideas, as well as for business and marketing. I was a little surprised by how it seemed to speed up the pace of life and everything else. Everything happens instantly. Everything seems urgent.”

“It was incredibly cool to be able to email someone, go to the library and read books and articles, chat with people from all over the world, and so on. At first, when I clicked on links from a system request, it also felt a bit like a club for the elite, where you could access all sorts of information and experiences that most people couldn't get simply because they didn't understand how the internet "worked.””

With all of that in mind I will conclude that it was varied from person to person. If someone liked working with computers and was in big city where internet was a thing than yes, most likely they liked internet all ideas it had. If you were in small town where internet was bourgeoisie thing to enjoy, then of course you will at least king of hate it. For example my father was in love with internet, he quickly realized how many benefits it could give and started using it, with that it was positive thinking. For my grandfather it was games and thing that will not last long, so more negative thoughts.
So let’s sum up all of that with fine words

In 2006, the number of Internet users exceeded 4 million. Internet providers began to provide access to the network using a "dedicated line", the speed increased, and the network became more similar to the web environment in which we live today. But it is worth noting an important feature: before social networks and instant messengers penetrated our everyday lives, using the Internet was much "more interesting", and it was done more thoughtfully.

There was an environment for each purpose. Those who wanted to share their thoughts registered on the LiveJournal platform, where a sense of security and freedom reigned, and quarrels in the comments were rather the exception to the rule. Those who craved communication sat in the legendary online chats, where they had to invent a nickname for themselves and try to find a common language in complete anonymity. For more in-depth communication on interests — from computers to music — there were forums where you had to monitor your statements and reputation, troublemakers were disliked and immediately "banned." Each site performed a certain function, while now we are all useless travelers on several crowded platforms of Facebook, YouTube, Twitter, and Instagram: we try to attract attention and solve all our problems at once, but we are heard less and less.
What it brought to people

With internet people were able to speak with their family in another city/country, be able to read more books, read more news, listen to music and more and more. But with that also worth mentioning impact of the internet on economy and other part of society.
Politics

In politics internet slowly but surely took place to influence elections. For example, it may be speculation as with all of the politics, but it is not a surprise that Zelenski won elections partial because of the fame in the internet. People want normal human to represent them and why not take person who played as president and in the movie did a pretty dissent job?
Here is what I am talking about, also what newspapers say:(take with a lot of grain of scepticism as a lot of the article may be also payed to talk about that so I will say it’s truth for 30%)

“Volodymyr Zelensky’s victory confirms that mastering social media and its features is one of the decisive factors for political success today. In contrast to Facebook and VK, it was on Instagram that we recorded the highest percentage of positive and neutral posts. One of the reasons is that such content is generated by numerous Ze accounts and fan pages. While other parties tried to catch up on what was lost on this social network with the start of the parliamentary election campaign, Servant of the People (SN) was far ahead.

Having completely mastered the social network with a reputation for being a platform about “frivolous things”, this party packaged politics into Instagram formats at the speed of light. Zelensky and his team’s hashtags were even used in posts about “European Solidarity”, “Fatherland” and “Voice”.

Vakarchuk's political force came late to the scene, but the fact that it made it to parliament underscores our thesis: large-scale involvement in social media can be an almost decisive part of an electoral strategy. Today, social media is also a political tool. Those who underestimated this weapon were left behind.”
Economic
If we are talking about how internet influenced out economy we should talk about first job to come in mind is IT, programmers and all sub categories of that profession named in Ukraine as IT.

IT, according to the results of 2022, the industry provided revenues to the Ukrainian economy in the amount of $ 7.35 billion or 4.5% of GDP (Fig. 2). The number of companies and enterprises in Ukraine that provide digital services is about 8.2 thousand, with 5 thousand of them looking for new employees, and about 1.5 thousand companies are technology startups. According to DOU, as of the end of February 2023, 271,699 individual entrepreneurs worked in the IT sector, which is 13.6% of all active individual entrepreneurs for that period (although some individual entrepreneurs may actually be employees, this is a significant share).
On the picture
on the top of the image: dynamics of IT export.

Than goes data vis with such information: in 2018 - 3204 billion dollars, in 2019 - 4173 billion dollars, in 2020 - 5 026 billion dollars, in 2021 - 6943 billion dollars, in 2022 - 7349 billion dollars.
In the end, at the bottom of picture they clarify that numbers wich goes up with the years pass by is export, billion dollars.

If we are talking globally then with help of the Internet, there are ATM's which can be used with online cards, Internet banking,  the ability to pay by phone, transfer money to another person just with click on the telephone and much more.
In fact, digital technologies and the World Wide Web have turned the entire financial industry upside down and created a whole range of new services and equipment that were impossible before.

With that also same happened to books and other instruction for learning something new, as only in the age of digital transformation has it become possible to sell the same product multiple times.You can buy an e-book, a knitting pattern, a photo, a song or a film - and the original will remain in the hands of the owner.
Digital maps instead of paper maps

People who live in big cities or love to travel cannot imagine their life without a map on their smartphone. After all, it not only shows routes, your location and the distance to your desired destination. Here you can see photos of any point on the planet that people have already visited, read reviews about establishments, find out about traffic jams, walk the streets of unknown cities.
Previously, we would ask passers-by how to get to this or that place, but now we can make a detailed itinerary, just holding the phone in our hands.
Remote work or studying online

This is one of the most favorite innovations of office workers and students, thanks to which you can work/study lying down, go to zoom meetings without pants and not spend 2-3 hours a day on the road.

This way of working leads to other changes - a decrease in the number of people in transport and the abandonment of offices, schools and universities. Already now, IT companies are reducing office space, because after the coronavirus epidemic, both employees and management liked working from home. Schools and Universities are encourage and often do same things, cause with that theu can take more students and it means more money.
However, not everyone is happy with such innovations. If you have three children, a dog, a parrot and two cats, then working or studying in different place is almost a vacation!
All of these are always affecting economics in one way or another.

And with that I will say that internet and it’s possibilities affect not only decisions of ordinary people but the ones who have authority as these people decided to do app(Дія or Dya is app for storing your passport and a lot more documents) which will stored all your necessary and a little bit of sensitive information but let’s talk about this in details.

The problems of informatization of state authorities, state organizations and enterprises are set out in the Decree of the President of Ukraine No. 663 of June 17, 1997 and the National Program of Informatization of Ukraine.

Despite all the problems, flaws, and existing limitations, the Internet is increasingly being used in government institutions. There are information system projects where technical solutions are made based on the capabilities and technologies of the Internet (in these systems, information does not fall into the category of restricted access).

The aforementioned Presidential Decree and the National Informatization Program provide for a number of tasks related to the creation of corporate networks of research institutions, ministries, and departments. Given the limited funding, the Internet may be the basic option for some projects.

The Diya application , introduced in Ukraine in 2020, allows you to store your driver's license, domestic and international passports and other documents in your smartphone , as well as transfer their copies when receiving banking or postal services, checking into a hotel and in other life situations. Also, through Diya (application and/or portal), you can receive such services as eMalyatko (a comprehensive service at the birth of a child), register a business and individual entrepreneur online, pay taxes and file declarations, sign any documents, change the place of registration, etc.

During 2019, mobile operators significantly expanded the coverage of the territory of Ukraine with 4G networks, which allowed to increase to 78% the share of the population that can receive mobile broadband Internet access services. At the same time, the expansion of the coverage area of ​​4G networks and the widespread distribution of consumer end devices running operating systems (smartphones, tablets) led to an increase in the volume of mobile Internet traffic consumption . In addition, the number of consumers of machine-to-machine ( M2M ) and Internet of Things ( IoT ) services continues to gradually increase. Thus, the provision of data transmission services, including Internet access, is becoming an increasingly key revenue item for mobile operators every year  .

With that expansion if the internet on December 17, 2024, the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine adopted in the first reading draft law No. 12094 on regulation in the field of electronic communications, which should ensure access to high-speed Internet in rural areas and on roads. The document provides for the establishment of standards for Internet connection speed outside large settlements. An important component of the draft law is the introduction of a communication quality control system  .
